2606.22475.title: All Green, Still Broken: Real-Flow Verification Lessons from an LLM-Integrated, Multi-Market Web Application
2606.22475.paper_summary: Modern web applications increasingly combine three ingredients that are hard to test: output from large language models, multi-market internationalization, and browser-driven front-ends over external data sources. We report on a production rental-search assistant whose automated suite grew to 1,553 test cases in six weeks. The suite passed continuously, yet user-facing defects continued to reach production. We studied all 252 bug-fix commits in the project and classified each by the boundary, or seam, it escaped through. About 44 percent of the fixes fall in four seams that component-level unit tests cannot observe: the live browser runtime, the non-default market, the end-to-end flow, and the whole-system level. A fix without a guard at the seam let one defect ship twice. We present the four-seam framework, the measured defect distribution, and the practices we adopted, including a simple way for a team to find the seam that carries the most fixes.
2606.22475.paper_summary_zh: 現代的網路應用程式越來越多地結合了三個難以測試的要素：大型語言模型的輸出、多市場國際化，以及基於外部數據來源的瀏覽器驅動前端。我們報告了一個生產租屋搜尋助手，其自動化測試套件在六週內增長至1,553個測試案例。該套件持續通過測試，但面向用戶的缺陷仍然不斷進入生產環境。我們研究了該專案中的252個修補提交，並根據缺陷逃逸的邊界或接縫進行了分類。約44%的修補位於四個組件級單元測試無法觀察到的接縫：實時瀏覽器運行時、非預設市場、端到端流程，以及整體系統層級。一個在接縫處沒有防護的修補讓一個缺陷重複進入生產。我們提出了四接縫框架、測量的缺陷分佈，以及我們採用的實踐，包括一種簡單的方法，讓團隊找到承載最多修補的接縫。
2606.22475.author: Muhammad Bilal et.al.
